old_claim.note: Basic Woodworking is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No woodworking dependency remains because the Ulucak loom is inferred from a weight cluster and no wooden frame survives in the scoped context.

source_shape.source_locator: Ulucak discussion calls the Building 12 group the clearest Neolithic loom evidence and identifies that evidence specifically as eleven loom weights.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The loom reconstruction rests on clustered tension weights; the source reports no preserved timber frame or woodworking trace from the installation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A plausible perishable frame is not enough to assert direct woodworking lineage when the node is intentionally locked to surviving evidence.

ontology_before: The inferred loom imported a generic woodworking predecessor despite no surviving frame evidence.
ontology_after: The Ulucak loom depends on its evidenced weights and textile function only.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: loom no longer directly depends on woodworking_basic.
would_reject_if: A wooden frame securely associated with the Building 12 weight cluster were recovered and analyzed.
short_reason: The scoped loom is inferred from weights, not preserved woodwork.
